prerequisite_chain.title: Foundations of Systems Thinking + AI — Full Prerequisite Chain
prerequisite_chain.author: Ricco Wilson

skills: Define system boundaries
Identify stocks, flows, and feedback loops
Draw causal loop diagrams (CLDs)
Distinguish reinforcing from balancing loops
Identify delays in system behavior

interactive_artifacts.description: Draw a CLD with 6+ variables, 1 reinforcing loop, 1 balancing loop, and 1 delay for a recurring business problem

skills: Map AI touchpoints onto a business system
Classify AI leverage types: prediction, automation, decision support
Identify bottlenecks and high-leverage nodes for AI placement
Design human-in-the-loop controls and monitoring

interactive_artifacts.description: Pick one business workflow, identify 3 AI leverage points, label each by type, and write a 1-sentence system impact hypothesis per point

chain.concept_name: SMB AI Mindset Shift (Tool Buying to System Redesign)
skills: Diagnose tool-first vs system-first AI adoption
Redesign workflows AI-first (roles, approvals, controls)
Write AI-first SOPs with explicit human checkpoints
Define outcome metrics vs activity metrics
Build escalation paths and quality standards for automated workflows

interactive_artifacts.description: Select one workflow, redesign it AI-first, and write a 10-bullet SOP including where AI acts, where humans check, and how exceptions escalate

skills: Identify reinforcing loops in AI and business systems
Label causal links with polarity (+ / -)
Determine loop type by counting negative links
Calculate loop gain and cycle time
Identify highest-leverage link for intervention

interactive_artifacts.description: Choose an AI system, identify one variable changing faster than expected, trace through 4+ variables, close the loop, and identify the highest-leverage intervention
MCP server CLD for AI costs: Agent Complexity > Token Usage > AI Spend > Team/Scaling Pressure > Agent Complexity. Two-point intervention at Token Usage and AI Spend projected 55% cost reduction.

mcp_tools.usage: Build CLDs to map reinforcing loop structures
Detect and classify reinforcing vs balancing loops in a CLD

skills: Score CLD nodes by loop count, position, and coupling
Rank intervention points using Meadows hierarchy (parameter vs structure vs goal)
Distinguish leverage from bottleneck
Perform accessibility checks on candidate leverage points
Design structural interventions vs parameter adjustments

interactive_artifacts.description: Score your CLD from ST-001, design two interventions at the top node (one parameter change, one structural), and argue why structural outperforms

AI operations CLD leverage scores: AI Spend (0.49), Team/Scaling Pressure (0.39), Token Usage (0.32), Agent Complexity (0.28). Budget cap (parameter) vs prompt caching + model tiering (structural) at same node.

mcp_tools.usage: Score and rank CLD nodes by leverage using loop count x average loop gain
Simulate perturbations at candidate leverage points to measure system output variance

skills: Recognize the Shifting the Burden archetype (B1, B2, R1 loop structure)
Classify fixes as symptomatic vs fundamental
Map erosion channels (architectural coupling, skill atrophy, resource diversion)
Design transition strategies that maintain symptomatic fix while building fundamental solution
Set concrete transition dates and measure fundamental solution viability over time

interactive_artifacts.description: Find a temporary fix running > 1 month, map B1/B2/R1, rate fundamental solution viability now vs when fix was first applied, and write a transition strategy
AI-powered EDI platform: human review queue as symptomatic fix, auto-generating training examples from corrections as fundamental solution bridge. Target: 50% queue reduction in 90 days.

mcp_tools.usage: Map the three-loop archetype structure (B1 symptomatic, B2 fundamental, R1 erosion)
Export the archetype diagram for team review and retrospectives

chain.domain: AI for Systems Thinking (Agentic Systems Design)
skills: Map agent architectures as causal loop diagrams
Identify reinforcing and balancing feedback loops in agent systems
Score loop severity in agent control flows
Detect retry storms and runaway agent behavior patterns
Design interventions to break harmful agent feedback loops

interactive_artifacts.description: Retry storm scenario: Agent routes request > Tool fails > Agent retries > Rate limit triggers > Timeout increases > Agent retries harder. Reinforcing loop with severity score and intervention recommendation.
Map your agent's feedback loops: Choose an AI agent system you use or are building. Map 3+ agent components as nodes, draw causal links, and identify at least one feedback loop.

mcp_tools.usage: Accepts agent components + links, returns loop classification with severity scores

chain.domain: AI for Systems Thinking (Agentic Systems Design)
skills: Input MCP tools and dependencies to build dependency graphs
Identify redundancy, coupling, and blast radius in tool stacks
Score tool interventions using Meadows hierarchy
Analyze tool stack health (complexity, redundancy, brittleness)
Design refactoring strategies for healthier tool architectures

interactive_artifacts.description: Realistic MCP tool stack with annotated leverage points: 5 interconnected tools showing redundancy in data-fetch tools, high coupling at the router, and blast radius analysis for the central orchestrator.
Audit your MCP tool stack: List 3-5 MCP tools you use, map their dependencies, identify the highest blast-radius tool, and score one intervention on the Meadows hierarchy.

mcp_tools.usage: Accepts tool list + dependencies + interventions, returns health scores and refactor recommendations

chain.domain: AI for Systems Thinking (Agentic Systems Design)
skills: Recognize shifting-the-burden archetype in AI automation contexts
Play scenario-based decision games to experience automation debt accumulation
Classify automation fixes as quick-fix vs fundamental investment
Detect erosion channels in automation workflows
Design transition strategies from symptomatic to fundamental automation solutions

interactive_artifacts.description: Three pre-loaded AI automation scenarios: (1) LLM low-confidence outputs routed to human review, (2) Agent edge-case failures handled by fallback scripts, (3) Tool call latency issues masked by caching layers.
Find automation debt in your workflow: Find one automation that started as a quick fix and is still running. Map the B1/B2/R1 loops and draft a transition strategy.

mcp_tools.usage: Accepts automation layers + fundamental solution, identifies burden shift patterns

foundations.description: Week 1 foundational track: from systems thinking basics through AI placement to AI-first operating model redesign

sequence.concept_name: SMB AI Mindset Shift (Tool Buying to System Redesign)

systems_thinking_deep.description: Intermediate systems thinking track: from feedback loop mechanics through leverage analysis to system archetype recognition

agentic_systems_design.description: Week 3 advanced track: apply systems thinking frameworks to agent architectures, tool stacks, and automation debt

cross_references.description: How the foundations track feeds into the systems thinking deep track and the agentic systems design track

bridges.relationship: W1-C1 (Systems Thinking Fundamentals) is the shared root. It enables W1-C2 in the foundations track and ST-001 in the deep track. Feedback loops, delays, and CLD concepts introduced in W1-C1 are prerequisite knowledge for both branches.

ST-001 (Reinforcing Feedback Loops) deepens the feedback loop concepts introduced in W1-C1. W1-C1 covers reinforcing and balancing loops at the foundational level; ST-001 formalizes polarity analysis, loop gain, and cycle time at the intermediate level.

W1-C2 introduces AI as a leverage point conceptually. ST-002 formalizes leverage point analysis with scoring, Meadows hierarchy, and sensitivity analysis. Learners who complete both tracks gain both intuitive and quantitative leverage thinking.
ST-004 extends feedback loop analysis from generic systems to agent-specific architectures. CLD skills from ST-001 are prerequisite.
ST-005 applies Meadows hierarchy from ST-002 to MCP tool stack interventions.
ST-006 applies shifting-the-burden archetype from ST-003 to AI automation debt.
ST-005 requires agent control flow understanding from ST-004 to analyze tool dependencies within agent architectures.

recommended_full_sequence.description: For learners completing all three tracks, the recommended order interleaves foundations, deep, and agentic content
recommended_full_sequence.rationale: Start with W1-C1 and W1-C2 to build foundational vocabulary, then ST-001 to deepen feedback loop mechanics before W1-C3 applies everything to SMB workflow redesign. ST-002 and ST-003 follow as the analytical capstone. ST-004/005/006 extend all three frameworks into the agentic AI domain.
